original: Battle of Sinhagad, Shivaji commander Tanaji Malusare recaptures fort of Sinhagad from the Mugals, scaling the walls with help of pet monitor lizard
Chimpanzee Nim Chimsky signs his 1st word, at 2½ months
Zara Lachlan becomes the first woman and youngest person to slow row from Europe to South America over 97 days (3,600 nautical-miles)
First successful face and double hand transplant announced performed on 22 year-old Joe DiMeo in New York
Chinese surveillance balloon shot down by US fighter jets off the US eastern seaboard, after drifting across the US for days and igniting a political storm
Russian space agency tests a 82-foot wide space mirror
Simulation video game "The Sims" developed by Maxis released in North America
First clinical trial approved to transfer genetically modified pigs kidneys into humans by the US Food and Drug Administration
Dutch speed skaterArd Schenkwins the 5,000m at the Sapporo Winter Olympics; also wins the 1,500m and 10,000m gold medals
Frank Aquilera sets then world record for distance of a frisbee flying disc toss of 551.2 ft (168m) in Las Vegas, Nevada
Clyde Tombaugh, American astronomer (discovered Pluto), born in Streator, Illinois (d. 1997)
Archaeologist Kent R. Weeks crawls inside KV5, the tomb of the sons of Ramases II, to discover the largest tomb ever found in the Valley of the Kings
First radioactive substance produced synthetically (radium E)
Ken Thompson, American computer scientist who developed UNIX, born in New Orleans, Louisiana[1]
Morocco's Mohammed VI switches on world's largest solar plant near Ouarzazate. Planned to power 1 million homes when fully completed 2018
Denmark approves plans for world's first energy island in the North Sea to provide power to 3 million Europeans
Bill Gatesgets a pie thrown in his face in Brussels, Belgium
Paris, France annuls 213-year-old law banning women from wearing trousers
Ludwig Prandtl, German physicist (considered the father of aerodynamics - discovered the boundary layer), born in Freising, Germany (d. 1953)
"Tetsuwan Atom" byOsamu Tezukafirst published in Weekly Shonen Magazine in Japan

Caitlin Clarkscores 60 points in a game for Dowling Catholic High School against Mason City High School, setting the second-highest single-game point total in Iowa five-on-five girls' basketball history
Super Bowl LII, US Bank Stadium, Minneapolis, MN: Philadelphia Eagles defeat New England Patriots, 41-33; MVP:Nick Foles, Philadelphia, QB
St. Jude Children’s Research Hospital opens in Memphis, Tennessee; funding for the cancer fighting facility led by entertainerDanny Thomas
Charles McGee, American retired US Air Force officer and fighter pilot of the Tuskegee Airmen promoted from colonel to brigadier general
Jonathan Larson, American theater composer (Rent), born in White Plains, New York (d. 1996)
Anders Holmertz swims world record 400 m freestyle (3:40.81)
Sandra Volker swims female European record 50m backstroke (27.77)
Soviet Union beats Canada, 2-0 at Cortina d'Ampezzo to clinch their first ever Olympic ice hockey gold medal; US takes silver, Canada bronze
NHL New York Islanders rookieMike Bossyscores 1st career hat trick in  6-1 win over Washington Capitols at Nassau Veterans Memorial Coliseum in Uniondale, New York
Baseball announces a special hall of fame wing for black players and coaches
Zimbabwe's 1st Test Cricket victory, over Pakistan by an inning
St Petersburg Pelicans beat West Palm Beach Tropics 12-4 to win 1st Senior Professional Baseball Association Championship
John Devitt, Australian swimmer (Olympic gold 4x200m freestyle relay 1956, 100m freestyle 1960; silver 100m freestyle 1956; International Swimming HOF), born in Sydney, Australia (d. 2023)
Sputnik 7 launches into Earth orbit; probable Venus probe failure
US launches Lunar Orbiter 3
AstronomersTycho BraheandJohannes Keplermeet for the first time near Prague
First US Electoral College choosesGeorge Washingtonas President andJohn Adamsas Vice President
French National Conventionproclaims abolition of slavery
Cyclone Batsirai strikes Madagascar less than a month after Cyclone Ana, causing widespread damage, killing at least 92 people and displacing 91,000
An earthquake measuring 6.1 on the Richter Scale in northeast Afghanistan kills more than 5,000
Tens of thousands of people are stranded by floods in the Australian states of New South Wales and Queensland
FAA begins 6 month test of reactions to sonic booms over Oklahoma City
Comic strip "Hagar The Horrible" by Dik Browne debuts
Post Maloneuploads his debut single "White Iverson" to his SoundCloud account
"Rumours" 11th studio album byFleetwood Macis released (Grammy Album of the Year)
Mario Lemieuxis 7th NHL player to score 600 goals
US Secretary of StateMadeleine Albrightannounces she just discovered that her grandparents were Jewish, three of whom were killed in the Holocaust
Jackie Robinsonbecomes the 1st African American executive of a major US TV and radio station as Director of Community Activities at radio WNBC-NY and TV station WNBT
Theodore "Ted" Stanley, American anesthesiologist and medical entrepreneur (created the fentanyl lollipop), born in Manhattan, New York (d. 2017)
Don Davis, American trombonist, arranger, and composer, mostly for film (The Matrix), and television (Beauty & The Beast), born in Anaheim, California
Florence LaRue, American pop and R&B vocalist (The 5th Dimension - "Up, Up, And Away"; "Aquarius/Let the Sunshine In"), born in Plainfield, New Jersey
El Anatsui, Ghanaian Sculptor (Bottle-top installations), born in Anyako, Ghana
Zenani Mandela, South African diplomat and daughter of Nelson and Winnie Mandela, born in Soweto, South Africa
Pamelyn Ferdin, American child actress (Happy Birthday Wanda June, THe Odd Couple (TV series); The Tool Box Murders), registered nurse, and animal rights activist, born in Los Angeles, California
James Dunn, American soul singer (The Stylistics - "Betcha by Golly, Wow"; "You Make Me Feel Brand New"), born in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania
Tsitsi Dangarembga, Zimbabwean writer (Nervous Conditions) and playwright, born in Mutoko, Southern Rhodesia
Denis Savard, Canadian NHL centre (Chicago Blackhawks) named one of the 100 Greatest NHL Players' in history, born in Pointe Gatineau, Canada
John Steel, British rock drummer (The Animals - "House of the Rising Sun"), born in Gateshead, County Durham, England
Marguerite "Marge" Ganser, American pop singer (The Shangri-Las - "Leader of the Pack"), born in Queens, New York City (d. 1996)
Mary Ann Ganser, American pop singer (The Shangri-Las - "Leader of the Pack"), born in Queens, New York City (d. 1970)
